title: Conjecture 1.3
statement_text: Every (even hole, $K_{4}$)-free graph of large enough treewidth contains the diamond.
context_text: Sintiari and Trotignon observed that their layered wheel graphs $G_{h,w}$ tend strongly to contain induced diamonds (the graph obtained from $K_4$ by deleting an edge) in order to maintain large treewidth while remaining even-hole-free, and conjectured that diamonds are unavoidable induced subgraphs in all (even hole, $K_4$)-free graphs of sufficiently large treewidth. The paper proves this conjecture as a special case of two stronger results that fully characterize which graphs $H$ must appear as induced subgraphs in such graphs.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.3 in full; more precisely, proves two stronger results: (a) for every $K_4$-free chordal graph $H$, every (even hole, $K_4$)-free graph of sufficiently large treewidth contains an induced copy of $H$ (the diamond case yields the conjecture), and (b) for every forest $H$ and every $t\in\mathbb{N}$, every even-hole-free graph of sufficiently large treewidth contains either $K_t$ or an induced subgraph obtained from $H$ by adding a universal vertex.
paper_title: Induced subgraphs and tree decompositions XI. Local structure in even-hole-free graphs of large treewidth
